_comment: NPC: Wang Ping (王平). Canon ID: N07. Per user directive: every miscellaneous NPC from RI must be accounted for. Prime Directive: Reality is objective; cultivation changes understanding, not existence. Every being described here exists independently of the player's perception.
name: Wang Ping

perception_tiers.nascent_soul: Same — mortal, but you sense his fragile health

note: Wang Lin's brother; tragic fate drives Wang Lin's early motivation

personality_traits._comment: INFERRED from canon: gentle younger brother, protective of family, willing to face hardship (fishing, firewood), worries but does not flee. Per Article XLI: general traits.

dao_heart.note: 0-100. A cracked Dao heart blocks breakthrough regardless of Qi. Per canon: Wang Lin's Dao heart is famously tested at every major realm.

initiation_lines: Brother, Da Niu wants to go fishing at the south bend. Will you come? We could use the extra food.
Father has been quiet lately. The Teng family's tax collectors came again yesterday. We gave them half our rice.
If the Heng Yue elder tests us, I hope you pass. I will stay and take care of Mother and Father, but you... you could become someone.

sect_tasks.description: Wang Ping grins: 'Da Niu and I were going to fish at the south bend. If you bring us some string for lines, I will share whatever we catch. Mother could use the extra food.'
Wang Ping looks at the dwindling woodpile: 'Winter will be hard this year. Father says we need more firewood. If you bring oak logs, I can help you split them.'

desires.what: Ask someone to help gather firewood before winter
desires.why: Per Wang Ping's initiation_lines and sect_tasks: 'Winter will be hard this year. Father says we need more firewood.' This is survival for a mortal family, not a quest.
desires.source: EXPLICIT: Derived from Wang Ping's sect_tasks 'chop_firewood' and initiation_lines. The firewood worry is canonical village life.
desires.line: The woodpile is low again. If you are heading to the eastern woods, bring back oak logs. I will help you split them and we can share the coal.

desires.what: Vent frustration about the Teng family's latest tax collection
desires.why: Per Wang Ping's initiation_lines: 'Father has been quiet lately. The Teng family's tax collectors came again yesterday. We gave them half our rice.' The eldest son internalizes the family's burden.
desires.source: EXPLICIT: Wang Ping's own initiation_lines reference the Teng tax. His personality_traits show loyalty: 0.95 — he would be angry on his family's behalf.
desires.line: They took half our rice again. Father did not say a word at dinner. He just stared at the table. I hate them.

desires.what: Invite someone to fish at the south bend with Da Niu
desires.why: Per Wang Ping's initiation_lines and sect_tasks 'fishing_south_bend': 'Da Niu and I were going to fish at the south bend.' Fishing is a normal village activity. The south bend is also where wolf attacks happen (living_events), making it simultaneously normal and dangerous.
desires.source: EXPLICIT: Wang Ping's initiation_lines: 'Da Niu wants to go fishing at the south bend.' His sect_tasks offer fishing.
desires.line: Da Niu and I are heading to the south bend to fish. Come with us — we could use the extra food, and it is safer in numbers.

desires.what: Follow Wang Lin to the hills to watch him practice
desires.why: Wang Ping is the youngest and curious. He idolizes his older brother. If the player is known to be heading toward the hills, he asks to come along.
desires.source: INFERRED: RI Ch.1. Wang Ping is the youngest brother. Younger siblings follow older ones.
desires.line: Wait for me! I want to come too!

desires.what: Ask someone who previously helped the family during the Teng tax crisis to help again
desires.why: Per Art XXXI.1 (DebtEngine — 'You owe me. Or I owe you. Both bind.'). The Teng family takes half the rice every season. Someone who helped the Wang family during the last crisis created a debt of gratitude. Wang Ping, loyal to a fault (loyalty: 0.95), remembers who helped and is not too proud to ask again. This is not a transaction — it is the social fabric of a poor village where mutual obligation is survival.
desires.source: INFERRED: RI Ch.1-3. The Teng tax oppression is explicit. Wang Ping's loyalty (0.95) and his initiation_lines about the tax ('They took half our rice again') establish the debt context. The specific act of calling in a favor is INFERRED, but consistent with village social dynamics where reciprocal obligation is the primary economy.
desires.line: You helped us before, when the Teng collectors came. We owe you for that. But... they are coming again next week. I do not know what we will do.
